name: Loot Generation
description: A procedural reward process that selects, rolls, filters, and grants loot from tables, context, rarity, and player state.
design_notes: Loot generation sits between randomization and economy balance. It needs generation_seed, loot_table_id, rng_stream_id, rarity_tier, duplicate_rule, pity_counter, inventory validation, and transaction logging so rewards are exciting, fair, and debuggable.
design_purpose: Create varied rewards from encounters, containers, rooms, or runs.
Control scarcity and progression through rarity, eligibility, and duplicate handling.
Support reproducible reward bugs and balance simulations.
player_fantasy: The player feels that rewards can surprise them while still fitting the activity, difficulty, and progression state.

parameters.name: loot_table_id
parameters.description: Reward table selected for the current context.

parameters.name: rng_stream_id
parameters.description: Named RNG stream used so loot rolls do not alter terrain or encounter generation.

parameters.name: rarity_tier
parameters.description: Selected or filtered reward tier before item choice and duplicate handling.

parameters.name: duplicate_rule
parameters.description: Rule applied when the selected reward is already owned or cannot be granted.

parameters.name: reward_transaction_id
parameters.description: Identifier used to prevent duplicate reward grants after retries or reconnects.

edge_cases: The selected item cannot be granted because inventory is full, item definition is missing, or duplicate_rule rejects it.
A deterministic run needs loot rolls to reproduce even when optional containers are skipped.
Player level, biome_id, and quest flags filter out every table entry.
A reward transaction succeeds but reveal UI or network response fails and is retried.
common_bugs: Loot rolls happen on both client and server and grant two different rewards.
Duplicate conversion grants currency but does not suppress the original item_instance_id.
Rarity filters are applied after item selection, making published odds inaccurate.
Reward_transaction_id is generated per UI retry instead of per committed reward.
balancing_notes: Balance expected value over many rolls and track actual drop distributions by loot_table_id.
Smart loot improves relevance but can reduce excitement if every reward is too predictable.
Pity and duplicate rules should reduce frustration without flooding the economy with high-tier rewards.
accessibility_notes: Show reward names, rarity labels, and duplicate conversion results in text as well as color or animation.
Allow reveal animations to be skipped, paused, or reduced for repeated loot drops.

implementation_notes.unity: Resolve loot_table_id with a deterministic RNG stream, validate inventory capacity and duplicate_rule, then commit reward_transaction_id before playing pickup or reveal effects.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent loot tables as Resources or JSON and run roll, filter, duplicate conversion, and inventory grant in one service so UI cannot duplicate rewards.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use server authority for multiplayer loot generation; log seed, table ID, selected entry, rarity_tier, and transaction ID for support and balance telemetry.
implementation_notes.web: Keep loot generation as a pure function of seed, table, context, and inventory snapshot, then commit a reward transaction diff before rendering results.

example_games.title: Diablo
example_games.note: Uses randomized loot drops as a major progression loop.

example_games.title: Borderlands
example_games.note: Uses generated weapon and loot rewards with rarity presentation.
